originalTitle: Ebola vaccine could take nine months as death toll rise further, WHO warns
title: Ebola vaccine could take nine months as death toll rise further, WHO warns
content: In its latest update, the World Health Organization says there have now been 139 suspected deaths and 600 cases.

originalTitle: Why does Ebola keep on occurring in DR Congo?
title: Why does Ebola keep on occurring in DR Congo?
content: Ebola was first discovered in what is now the DR Congo in 1976 and the country is now facing its 17th outbreak.

originalTitle: Nigeria arrests former minister in hiding after corruption conviction
title: Nigeria arrests former minister in hiding after corruption conviction
content: The former power minister was sentenced to 75 years in jail in absentia earlier this month.

originalTitle: Iran claims it coordinated passage of 26 vessels out of Hormuz in 24 hours
title: Iran claims it coordinated passage of 26 vessels out of Hormuz in 24 hours
content: The IRGC claims to maintain control over the Strait of Hormuz despite the US blockade on Iran's ports.

originalTitle: At least eight killed in Israel’s air attacks on southern Lebanon
title: News from Far Away: Hoping for Peace!
content:
Hello from Africa!

Jambo, my amazing friends across Africa! It's your friendly news reporter here, sharing big news from our wonderful continent and sometimes from faraway lands too. Today, we're talking about something important: how everyone wants to be safe and happy, no matter where they live. We always hope for peace in the world, like a calm and sunny day after a storm.

What's Happening Far Away?

Far, far away from us, in a place called the Middle East, there are two countries called Israel and Lebanon. They are neighbors, just like you might have neighbors next door. Sometimes, neighbors have little disagreements. But sometimes, countries can have bigger disagreements. This can make people feel worried and not safe. Did you know? Lebanon is a country with tall mountains and a beautiful sea. It has very old cities too! Israel also has old, old cities and hot, sandy deserts in some parts. Both places are home to many children, just like you, who love to play and learn!

What is a 'Ceasefire'?

Imagine you are playing your favorite game with your friends, and maybe you start to argue a little bit. What happens then? Maybe a grown-up says, 'Okay, everyone, take a timeout!' A 'ceasefire' is a bit like a timeout for countries. It's a big, important promise. It means that the grown-ups in charge agree to stop their disagreements for a little while. They say, 'Let's pause and try to talk instead of fight.' This timeout is super important because it helps people feel safe. It means children can go to school, grown-ups can go to work, and everyone can just live their lives without being scared. Recently, Israel and Lebanon had a ceasefire, or a timeout, which was supposed to last until July. This was good news because it meant people could have a break from feeling worried. Everyone hoped the timeout would make things better and lead to long-lasting peace. Did you know? The word 'ceasefire' is a big word, but it just means 'stop the fighting!' It's a way for countries to try and be friends again, like when you make up with a friend after an argument.

Why is Peace Important?

When countries are peaceful, everything is better! Children can laugh and play outside. They can learn new things at school without worry. Families can be together and feel safe in their homes. Peace means that people can build new things and help each other. But sometimes, even during a timeout, problems can still happen. We heard that some sad things happened in that area, and some people got hurt. This makes everyone feel very, very sad. It reminds us that even when people promise to have a timeout, it can be hard to make sure everyone is safe all the time. Our hearts go out to all the children and families there. Did you know? Children all over the world, from Africa to the Middle East, want the same things: to be loved, to be safe, and to have fun!

Hoping for a Happy Tomorrow

Many grown-ups around the world, and many big organizations, work very hard to help countries become friends. They want the timeouts to work and for the disagreements to stop for good. They want people to live in peace and harmony forever. It’s a very big job, but they keep trying. We in Africa know how important peace is. We always hope that all countries can learn to live together, share, and be good neighbors. We send our warmest wishes to the children and families in Israel and Lebanon, hoping they can soon live without any worries. Did you know? There's a big club of countries called the United Nations, and one of their main jobs is to help keep peace around the world. They are like a super team trying to make everyone friends!

What Can We Do?

Even though we are far away, we can still hope for peace. We can learn about other countries and understand that everyone wants to be safe. We can be kind to our friends and family here in Africa, showing how good it is when people are kind to each other. Every little bit of kindness helps the world become a better place! Remember, even when things are difficult, people always hope for peace, like hoping for the sun to shine after a rainy day. Let's keep hoping for bright, peaceful days for everyone, everywhere. Thank you for listening, my wonderful friends! Stay safe and keep smiling!
summary: Countries far away, Israel and Lebanon, are trying to have a 'timeout' called a ceasefire to stop disagreements, and everyone hopes for lasting peace and safety for all children and families.
whyMatters: This matters because when countries are peaceful, all children can be safe, go to school, and play without worry, which helps everyone live a happy life.

title: Southampton hit out at 'disproportionate' Spygate punishment

title: Saka calls out critics as players celebrate title at Emirates at 5am
content: Arsenal have had to wait 22 years to celebrate a title win, and both the club's players and fans made sure to savour every second of it.

originalTitle: Emery eyes fifth Europa crown but denies being 'king' of competition
title: Emery eyes fifth Europa crown but denies being 'king' of competition
